arts.sum: The international break spawned a social media phenomenon as the ‘Barclaysmen’ trend exploded in football circles. arts.ttl: Manchester City v Inter: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: The 2024/25 Champions League season kicks off with a blockbuster matchup as Manchester City hosts Inter Milan, rekindling memories of their 2023 final encounter. This high-profile fixture marks the start of the new league phase format in Europe’s premier club competition. Simone Inzaghi’s Inter side enters the match following a 1-1 draw with Monza in Serie A, while Pep Guardiola’s City team comes in with a perfect record of four wins from four Premier League games, most recently defeating Brentford. The revamped Champions League format has done away with the traditional group stage. arts.ttl: Why are Manchester City playing in white at home against Inter Milan in the Champions League?
arts.sum: Manchester City are set to change out of their normal home shirt when they take on Inter Milan in the new format of the Champions League, with the English side debuting a strip co-designed by Oasis singer Noel Gallagher . The Gallagher brothers are known to be huge Manchester City fans and Noel can often be seen attending games at the Etihad Stadium.

arts.ttl: John Terry responds to Rodri strike threat with clear Club World Cup stance

arts.ttl: Sommer: “Manchester City are not only Erling Haaland”
arts.sum: Inter Milan goalkeeper Yann Sommer expressed his eagerness and confidence ahead of the team’s upcoming Champions League match against Manchester City. In a pre-match press conference, Sommer shared his thoughts on the team’s preparation, their start to the season, and the challenge of facing one of Europe’s top teams. “I can’t wait to play tomorrow’s game. We are prepared, we have worked to the maximum for this type of match, and I’m looking forward to it,” Sommer stated, highlighting the team’s readiness for the high-profile encounter. arts.ttl: Sneijder: “My ranking: Inter are in the front row with City and Real Madrid”
arts.sum: Former Inter midfielder Wesley Sneijder believes the Nerazzuri have what it takes to win the Champions League this season. Speaking with La Gazzetta dello Sport, the Dutchman previewed his former clubs match with Manchester City, explaining with Simone Inzaghi can match Pep Guardiola tonight. So Wesley, do you like this new Champions League? “I come from another generation, I played my whole life thinking of facing the same teams at home and away, but a novelty always excites me.
